front: Installation: Wrapped the Reichstag building in Berlin with polypropylene fabric (1995); blue rope; over 100,000 square meters
Installation: 7,503 saffron-colored fabric panels hung from gates along 23 miles of footpaths in Central Park (2005)
Installation: Yellow umbrellas in California and blue umbrellas in Japan simultaneously; one blew loose and killed a bystander
Installation: An 18-foot-high white nylon fence running through California; documented in a Maysles film
Installation: 11 islands in Biscayne Bay, Miami surrounded with bright pink floating fabric
Artist: Annie Leibovitz photographed this artist wrapped in fabric in Central Park — a reference to his lifelong obsession
Artist: Bulgarian-born mononymic environmental artist; worked in collaboration with wife Jeanne-Claude; died May 2020
Artist: Part of the Nouveau réalisme movement founded by Yves Klein; decades-long project Mastaba planned for UAE
